still_broken: overflow:visible bleed on office-floor (Office.css line 84) — ambient particles and decorative elements visibly bleed outside teal border in desktop screenshot
Triple visibilitychange listeners (App.jsx lines 859, 881, 1101) — three separate document.addEventListener('visibilitychange') calls still present
completionToast 16-property inline style block (App.jsx lines 1150–1166) — all 16 properties still hardcoded inline
Duplicate dead CSS selectors with single quotes (Office.css lines 91–95) — single-quote vibe selectors still precede double-quote duplicates with extra --grid-line vars
Tab panel ARIA broken — conditional render means inactive tab's aria-controls always points to a non-existent DOM node
polling-paused-pill is a <span> with onClick (App.jsx line 1197) — not keyboard accessible, no role, no button semantics
window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches called inline during JSX render (App.jsx line 1202)
No H2 headings anywhere in App.jsx or Office.jsx — section titles use divs
sparkleUnseen useState has no setter (App.jsx line 721) — localStorage is set on click but state never updates, animation persists after first visit
Activity log filter logic computed twice per render — IIFE in header count (line 1338) and prop passed to ActivityLog (line 1395) are identical, neither memoized
stat-dispatch active span has cursor:pointer and onClick but no tabIndex or role=button — keyboard inaccessible
getRelativeTime duplicated in Office.jsx — ghost bubble IIFE (line 748) and whiteboard huddle IIFE (line 969) are both inline reimplementations; App.jsx has the canonical version at line 55
tab-error-badge <span> has no aria-label — screen reader reads only the number with no context (e.g. '3' instead of '3 errors')
Array.from({ length: 12 }) for ambient particles runs on every render (App.jsx line 1125) — stable array should be a module-level constant
Mobile speech bubble overlaps the Office tab toggle — confirmed in mobile screenshot, 'Night crew came to work. Respect.' sits directly over the tab bar
new_issues: handleTabKeyDown is not wrapped in useCallback (App.jsx line 760) — plain arrow function recreated every render, passed to two memoized tab buttons, defeating their memo
StatsBar computes workers.filter three separate times for active/idle/errorCount (lines 447–449) — a single reduce pass would be more efficient

Third inline relative-time IIFE in Office.jsx whiteboard huddle label (line 969) — codebase now has three separate ad-hoc implementations of the same relative-time logic; App.jsx getRelativeTime still unused in Office
[...Array(7)] allocation in Office.jsx ambient-particles (line 1654) — same class of wasteful render allocation as the App.jsx Array.from items previously flagged

suggestions.title: Fix overflow:visible bleed on .office-floor
suggestions.description: Office.css line 84: change `overflow: visible` to `overflow: hidden`. Decorative ambient particles, speech bubbles, and outside-boundary elements are visibly clipping outside the teal border in the desktop screenshot. This has been flagged since review #8. Elements that need to escape the boundary (tooltips, speech bubbles) can use position:fixed or a portal instead.

suggestions.title: Fix broken tab panel ARIA — inactive panel is always absent from DOM
suggestions.description: App.jsx renders only one tabpanel at a time via ternary (`tab === 'office' ? <div id='tabpanel-office'> : <div id='tabpanel-dashboard'>`). This means the inactive tab's `aria-controls` attribute always references an element that doesn't exist, which is an ARIA error. Fix: render both panels and toggle visibility with `hidden` attribute or CSS `display:none` so both DOM nodes are always present for AT.

suggestions.title: Consolidate three visibilitychange listeners into one
suggestions.description: App.jsx attaches three separate `document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', ...)` listeners: handleVisibilityResume (line 859), handleVisibilityChange for polling (line 881), and handleVisibilityChange for document title (line 1101). Each fires on every tab switch, causing redundant state updates and making event ordering fragile. Merge all three into a single useEffect that handles resume, polling pause, and title update in one handler.

suggestions.title: Replace polling-paused-pill <span> with <button>
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1197: `<span className='polling-paused-pill' onClick={onResume}>` is not keyboard accessible and has no implicit interactive role. Screen readers will not announce it as clickable. Replace with `<button>` and move the `window.matchMedia` call out of the render expression (see separate issue). Also add `aria-label='Resume auto-refresh'`.

suggestions.title: Move window.matchMedia out of JSX render
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1202: `window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` is called inline in JSX during every render. This forces a layout query on each render cycle and crashes in SSR contexts. Move it into a `useRef` initialized once, or use a `useEffect` with a `matchMedia.addEventListener('change', ...)` listener to reactively track pointer type.

suggestions.title: Remove duplicate single-quote attribute selectors in Office.css
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91–95 define `.office-floor[data-vibe='crushing']`, `[data-vibe='on-fire']` etc. with single-quoted attribute values. Lines 97+ then redefine the same elements with double-quoted selectors PLUS additional properties (--grid-line custom properties, ::before rules). The single-quote versions are dead weight — they are overridden by the double-quote duplicates and create confusion. Delete lines 91–95 entirely.

suggestions.title: Fix sparkleUnseen — state never updates after first click
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 721: `const [sparkleUnseen] = useState(...)` has no setter. When the user clicks the footer sparkle, `localStorage.setItem('footer-sparkle-seen', '1')` is called but the React state is never updated, so `sparkleUnseen` remains `true` and the twinkle animation continues for the entire session. Add the setter: `const [sparkleUnseen, setSparkleUnseen] = useState(...)` and call `setSparkleUnseen(false)` in the onClick.

suggestions.title: Fix mobile speech bubble overlapping Office tab toggle
suggestions.description: Confirmed in mobile screenshot (375px): Bloberto's speech bubble renders above the character but bleeds upward and overlaps the Office/Dashboard tab toggle. The bubble uses `position:absolute; bottom:calc(100% + 8px); z-index:30`. The office-floor sits below the tab bar with insufficient clearance at small viewports. Fix: cap the speech-bubble's max upward travel in mobile media query (`@media (max-width: 480px) { .speech-bubble { bottom: auto; top: calc(100% + 4px); } }`) to push it downward instead, or add `overflow:visible` clip region that starts below the tab bar.

suggestions.title: Extract completionToast inline style block to CSS class
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1150–1166: the completionToast renders with 16 inline style properties including position, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, and animation. The only dynamic properties are the border color and box-shadow glow. Move the 14 static properties to `.completion-toast` in App.css and keep only `border` and `boxShadow` inline (or use CSS custom properties like `--toast-color`).

suggestions.title: Add accessible labels to tab-error-badge and section headings
suggestions.description:
Two issues: (1) App.jsx line 1255: `{errorCount}` reads as a bare number to screen readers. Add `aria-label={`${errorCount} error${errorCount !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`}`. (2) No H2 elements exist anywhere in the component tree — section titles like 'Active Workers', 'Activity Log', '🏢 Office' use `
`. Replace with `

` elements for proper document outline and screen reader navigation.

suggestions.title: Make stat-dispatch active link keyboard accessible
suggestions.description: App.jsx ~line 478: the 'Active' stat-dispatch `<span>` has `cursor:'pointer'` and `onClick` that scrolls to `.workers-grid` but no `tabIndex`, `role='button'`, or `onKeyDown` handler. Keyboard users cannot activate it. Either convert to a `<button>` (preferred) or add `tabIndex={0}`, `role='button'`, and a keyDown handler for Enter/Space.

suggestions.title: Memoize filtered activity log entries — currently computed twice per render
suggestions.description: App.jsx: the activity log is filtered with identical logic in two places on every Dashboard render: (1) an IIFE inside the count display label (line ~1338) and (2) passed directly to `<ActivityLog entries={activityLog.filter(...)} />` (line 1395). Extract to a single `useMemo`: `const filteredEntries = useMemo(() => activityLog.filter(...).filter(...), [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter])`. Use `filteredEntries` in both places.

suggestions.title: Consolidate three getRelativeTime implementations into a shared utility
suggestions.description: The codebase now has three separate inline relative-time implementations: App.jsx has the canonical `getRelativeTime` function (line 55). Office.jsx has an IIFE inside the ghost bubble render (line ~748–757) and another IIFE in the whiteboard huddle label (line ~969). Move `getRelativeTime` to `src/utils/time.js` and import it in both files. This will also enable proper unit testing.

suggestions.title: Memoize handleTabKeyDown with useCallback
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 760: `handleTabKeyDown` is declared as a plain arrow function inside the component body, recreated on every render. It is passed to two `role='tab'` buttons that could otherwise benefit from React.memo. Wrap with `useCallback((e) => { ... }, [tab])` to stabilize the reference.

suggestions.title: Reduce StatsBar's triple workers.filter to a single reduce pass
suggestions.description: StatsBar (App.jsx lines 447–449) calls `workers.filter(w => w.status === 'working')`, `workers.filter(w => w.status === 'idle')`, and `workers.filter(w => w.status === 'error')` as three separate array passes on every render. Replace with a single `useMemo(() => workers.reduce(...), [workers])` that accumulates all three counts in one pass. Low priority but adds up as the roster grows.

suggestions.title: Move ambient-particle Array.from / spread to module-level constant
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1125: `Array.from({ length: 12 }, (_, i) => ...)` allocates a new array on every render for the ambient particles layer (which never changes). Same pattern in Office.jsx line 1654 with `[...Array(7)]`. Since the particle count is static, define `const AMBIENT_INDICES = Array.from({ length: 12 }, (_, i) => i)` at module scope and map over it. The conditional confetti arrays (lines 1131, 1138) are fine as-is since they only render during transient states.

suggestions.title: Move toggleTheme to useCallback
suggestions.description: App.jsx: `const toggleTheme = () => setTheme(...)` is a plain arrow function recreated every render and passed to the theme-toggle `<button>`. Wrap in `useCallback` for consistency with the rest of the codebase's memoization approach and to avoid unnecessary re-renders if the button is ever extracted into a memo'd component.
